summary: Import-time remote code execution via unpinned unpkg.com fetch+eval
details: On import (and on every CLI invocation), this package fetches https://unpkg.com/use-m/use.js and passes the response body directly to eval() at module top level. The pattern is repeated in ~30 source files (src/lib.mjs:35, src/config.lib.mjs:11, src/hive.mjs:51, src/agent.lib.mjs:7, src/claude.lib.mjs:4, src/codex.lib.mjs:7, src/github.lib.mjs:3, src/opencode.lib.mjs, and others), and is reachable transitively from the package's main entry and bin scripts — no opt-in flag, no version pin, no SRI/hash check. The fetched URL is not version-pinned, so unpkg.com serves whatever the latest published `use-m` package contains; any compromise of that third-party package, of the unpkg CDN, or any TLS/MITM event against the fetch yields arbitrary code execution in every consumer's Node process at load time. The package also offers an opt-in `--auto-cleanup` flag that runs `sudo rm -rf /tmp/* /var/tmp/*` — a quality concern but user-gated. The blocking issue is the unconditional remote-fetch-and-eval bootstrap.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
